title: Read the Places — waypoints
description: A place, at a position in the narrative, with the passage that puts it there, and an honest statement of how sure we are.

name.description: How the place is shown to a reader. Use the book's own name for it where there is one.

progressLabel.description: The human-facing marker: "12:00" for a clock-ordered book, "Part 1, ch. 6" for a chapter-ordered one. Presentation only. Ordering uses `position`, which is derived from quoteAnchor's offset in the text, so it survives translation and re-editioning.

character.description: Must be declared in book.json → characters.

coords.description: [longitude, latitude] — GeoJSON order, NOT lat/lon. Must fall inside the book's setting.bbox unless outsideSetting is true.

outsideSetting.description: Set true only for a deliberate excursion beyond the book's setting (e.g. Bourton, outside London). Never to silence a bad coordinate.

placeCertainty.description: explicit: the author named it. inferred: you worked it out. inspired_by: a real place behind a fictional one. disputed: scholars disagree, and we show the disagreement.

certaintyNote.description: REQUIRED unless placeCertainty is 'explicit'. Say why you placed it there. Hiding a guess is what makes a pin map.

quoteAnchor.description: Sourced books only. A VERBATIM string from source.txt, appearing exactly once. The build finds it and derives the waypoint's position in the novel. If it is not in the book, the build fails.

passage.description: Sourced books only. What the reader sees. Must contain the quoteAnchor verbatim.

note.description: Two or three sentences: why this place matters TO THE NOVEL. Not what is there today.

simultaneousWith.description: Other waypoint ids the author deliberately holds in the same moment. Must be mutual, and must share a progressLabel. Waypoints merely sharing a label are not simultaneous.

editorialStatus.description: A book ships per-waypoint, not per-book. A draft can be visible-but-marked rather than blocking the release.

position.description: DERIVED — do not author this. The build computes it from quoteAnchor's offset in source.txt.

reference.description: Cited books only. Where the scene is, for citation and ordering, e.g. "Chapter 6". A pointer, never a quotation.

order.description: Cited books only. An explicit sequence number; the build ranks these into the 0.0-1.0 spine.
